title: IoT Device Template Schema
description: Canonical schema for IoT device template manifests. backend/device-template-schema.json is the authoritative structural contract for custom template import, AI add_template, and default template resources; runtime then applies DTO validation, BoardStorageServiceImpl.validateTemplateManifestForNuSmv(), and a NuSMV probe pre-check for semantic constraints.

Name.description: Device template name (will be sanitized for NuSMV via sanitizeSmvToken: spaces removed, non-alphanumeric replaced with '_')

Modes.description: Operating modes for multi-mode devices (e.g., ['ThermostatFanMode', 'ThermostatMode']). Because this schema validates raw manifests at the API/AI/default-template boundary, each mode must already be a valid NuSMV identifier before submission; use 'ThermostatMode' rather than 'Thermostat Mode'. Runtime NuSMV validation repeats normalization checks as a defensive semantic pass.

InitState.description: Initial state. For single-mode: 'stateName'. For multi-mode: 'mode1State;mode2State'. Empty string or '_' for wildcards allowed.

WorkingStates.description: All possible working states of the device

Name.description: State name. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated (e.g., 'auto;cool'). Spaces are removed by sanitizeSmvToken (e.g., 'emergency heat' -> 'emergencyheat').

Dynamics.description: Variable changes in this state. Can be empty array if no changes. Each item must have either ChangeRate or Value.

ChangeRate.description: Integer change rate per time unit (mutually exclusive with Value)

Value.description: Direct value assignment (mutually exclusive with ChangeRate)

Trust.description: Trust level (will be normalized to lowercase)

Privacy.description: Privacy level (will be normalized to lowercase)

InternalVariables.description: Device internal variables and environment variables

Name.description: Variable name (must be valid NuSMV identifier). Runtime validates against full NUSMV_RESERVED_WORDS set (case-insensitive).

IsInside.description: true = device-internal variable, false = environment variable (shared across devices). NOTE: Runtime treats null as false (environment variable), not true. Always set explicitly.

PublicVisible.description: Whether variable is visible to external observers

Values.description: Enumerated values (mutually exclusive with LowerBound/UpperBound). Spaces in values will be removed by sanitizeSmvToken (e.g., 'fan only' -> 'fanonly').

LowerBound.description: Minimum value for integer range (requires UpperBound)

UpperBound.description: Maximum value for integer range. Attack mode scaling is handled automatically by SmvBoundsUtils.resolveEffectiveUpperBound().

NaturalChangeRate.description: Natural change rate: single value '1' or range '[-1, 1]'

ImpactedVariables.description: List of environment variables this device can affect. Runtime validates as NuSMV identifiers (no spaces, no reserved words) via validateSmvIdentifier().

Transitions.description: Automatic state transitions triggered by conditions (non-signal by default). Found in 9 templates: Camera, Mobile Phone, Email, Facebook, Home Mode, Online Bank, Sina Weibo, Twitter, Clock.

Name.description: Transition name. If Signal=true, a boolean variable is generated (sanitized via formatApiSignalName: non-alphanumeric -> '_')

StartState.description: Starting state. Empty string '' means 'from any state'. '_' is wildcard. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated partial patterns allowed (e.g., ';sendingAlertMessage').

EndState.description: Target state after transition. Optional: if omitted, null, or empty, no state change occurs but assignments still execute (e.g., Clock.reset only resets variables). For multi-mode: semicolon-separated. Partial changes like ';idle' allowed.

Trigger.description: Condition that triggers this transition. Required for non-signal transitions; validated by SmvModelValidator.validateTrigger().

Attribute.description: Variable or mode name to check

Relation.description: Comparison operator. EQ/NEQ/GT/GTE/LT/LTE/== are normalized to =/!=/>/>=/</<= by SmvRelationUtils.normalizeTriggerRelation().

Value.description: Value to compare against (spaces will be removed by generator)

Assignments.description: Variable assignments when transition occurs. Validated by SmvModelValidator.validateAssignments().

Signal.description: If true, creates a boolean signal variable for this transition. Typically false for transitions (default).

APIs.description: External APIs that can be called to control the device via rule commands

Name.description: API name (spaces allowed). Signal variable name is generated via formatApiSignalName: non-alphanumeric chars (including spaces) replaced with '_', then appended with '_a'. E.g., 'take photo' -> signal variable 'take_photo_a'.

StartState.description: Starting state constraint. Empty string '' (default) means 'from any state' (22 of 29 API-bearing templates). Non-empty StartState found in 7 templates: Camera ('on','off'), Mobile Phone ('on'), Email ('idle'), Facebook ('idle'), Online Bank ('idle'), Sina Weibo ('idle'), Twitter ('idle'). For multi-mode: semicolon-separated partial patterns allowed.

EndState.description: Target state after API call. All 45 existing templates have non-empty EndState. For multi-mode: semicolon-separated. Partial changes like ';cool' or 'auto;' allowed.

Trigger.description: Always null for APIs (triggers are for Transitions only)

Assignments.description: Variable assignments when API is called. The canonical schema validates the assignment shape; NuSMV generation applies additional semantic checks where supported.

Signal.description: If true, creates a boolean signal variable '<name>_a' for this API. All 45 existing templates use Signal=true for APIs. Signal=true is required for the API to be usable as a rule condition (relation=null, value=null pattern). Signal=false APIs can only be used as rule command actions, not conditions. NOTE: DTO default is null (falsy), not true; omitting this field means no signal variable is generated. Always set explicitly.

Contents.description: Content/data items managed by the device (e.g., photos, data). Used for privacy checking in specifications.

$comment: Validation Notes: 1) Empty StartState ('') is intentional for APIs callable from any state (22/29 API-bearing templates); 7 templates have non-empty API StartState. 2) Name sanitization differs by context: sanitizeSmvToken removes spaces then replaces non-alnum with '_'; formatApiSignalName replaces non-alnum (including spaces) with '_' then appends '_a'. 3) NuSMV reserved words are rejected case-insensitively by runtime validation. 4) Trust/Privacy values are normalized to lowercase. 5) Multi-mode states use semicolon separator (e.g., 'mode1;mode2'). 6) Partial state changes use empty parts (e.g., ';cool' changes only mode2). 7) InternalVariable/ImpactedVariable names must not contain spaces (rejected at validation time). 8) InternalVariable and ImpactedVariable are allowed to have the same name (common pattern: device internal var affects environment var). 9) Generator supports state relation operators = != in 'not in' for state attributes; > < >= <= only for internal variables. 10) Transition.EndState is optional: when absent, no state change occurs but assignments (e.g., variable resets) still execute (see Clock.json). 11) Schema limits that are intentionally paired with runtime checks: (a) Reserved word checks are described in schema and enforced case-insensitively at runtime. (b) Modes must be legal identifiers in raw manifests; runtime repeats mode normalization/collision checks as a defensive semantic pass. (c) APIs.Trigger is constrained to null because API triggers have no generator semantics.
